senses.definition: lacking moisture or liquid

examples.x: She watered the dry plants in the garden.
The desert sand was dry and coarse beneath their feet.
The towel felt dry to the touch after being hung out in the sun.
Her lips were dry and cracked from exposure to the wind.
After the rain stopped, the pavement quickly became dry under the heat.

senses.definition: (of food) having a firm, dry texture that makes a sharp, crunching sound when broken or bitten
(of food) containing or cooked in a lot of oil
showing a smooth, soft, and luxurious quality similar to the feel of velvet fabric
having a sweet taste, often resembling or containing sugar

examples.x: The sugary donut coated in powdered sugar melted in her mouth, satisfying her sweet craving.
The sugary icing on the birthday cake added an extra layer of sweetness to the celebratory dessert.
The sugary cereal was a favorite among children, offering a sweet start to their day.
The sugary glaze on the freshly baked cinnamon rolls gave them a delectable sweetness.
The chocolate truffles were rolled in sugary cocoa powder, intensifying their rich and sweet flavor.

senses.definition: having the sweet and rich taste or qualities of honey

examples.x: The dessert featured a honeyed drizzle over the warm apple pie, enhancing its sweetness.
The honeyed sweetness of the barbecue sauce glazed over the grilled chicken created a mouthwatering experience.
The pastry chef skillfully crafted a honeyed syrup to drizzle over the warm baklava, intensifying its sweetness.
The honeyed dressing on the spinach salad balanced the flavors with a touch of sweetness.
The honeyed marinade on the grilled peaches brought out their natural sweetness, making them a delightful dessert.

senses.definition: damaged or altered by exposure to excessive heat or fire, resulting in a charred or blackened appearance and often an undesirable flavor

examples.x: The burned cookies were dark and crispy from staying in the oven too long.
The burned paper had black edges from getting too close to the candle.
Touching something hot left him with a burned hand.

senses.definition: heavily seasoned with spices and then cooked at high heat until the spices form a dark, crusty coating on the surface of the food
(of food, particularly meat) hard to chew or cut

examples.x: The steak was so tough, it was almost impossible to chew.
The bread was too tough to eat without soaking it in water.
The tough meat took longer to break down with each bite.
The tough chicken breast made it hard to enjoy the meal.
The pizza crust was too tough for my young child to chew.

senses.definition: providing essential nutrients

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, they enjoyed a hearty stew that was both warming and filling.
The restaurant is known for its hearty breakfasts, which include large portions of eggs, bacon, and toast.
She prepared a hearty casserole for the family dinner, packed with meat, vegetables, and cheese.
The hearty soup was perfect for a cold winter day, offering both comfort and nourishment.
They served a hearty roast beef with all the trimmings at the holiday feast, satisfying everyone's appetite.

senses.definition: having a soft and pulpy texture, often lacking firmness

examples.x: The wet sand beneath her feet felt mushy and unstable.
The ripe banana had a mushy texture, perfect for baking.
The leftover pasta became mushy after being stored in the fridge for too long.
The mashed potatoes were creamy and mushy, perfect for the dish.
Overcooked broccoli can become mushy and lose its vibrant color.

senses.definition: (of food) easy to chew or cut

examples.x: The steak was so tender that it practically melted in my mouth.
The braised lamb shanks were incredibly tender, falling off the bone with ease.
They marinated the pork overnight to make it extra tender and flavorful.
The vegetables in the stew were cooked to perfection, tender but not mushy.

senses.definition: weighing very little
having  a rich, creamy, and smooth flavor similar to butter

examples.x: Her homemade cookies were buttery, with a soft and chewy consistency.

senses.definition: (of food) requiring to be chewed a lot in order to be swallowed easily

examples.x: The chewy caramel candies stuck to her teeth as she savored their sweetness.
He enjoyed the chewy texture of the baguette, complemented by a crispy crust.
The beef jerky was tough and chewy, providing a satisfying snack on the go.

senses.definition: having a smooth and soft texture

examples.x: The soup had a creamy texture, rich and velvety on the palate.
She spread the creamy peanut butter onto the slice of bread.
The lotion left her skin feeling soft and creamy.
The mashed potatoes were whipped to a creamy consistency.

senses.definition: easily breaking into small pieces when pressed

examples.x: The crumbly texture of the cookie made it perfect for dipping into milk.

senses.definition: firm and making a crisp sound when pressed, stepped on, or chewed

examples.x: The crunchy texture of the fresh vegetables added a satisfying contrast to the salad.
The crunchy apples were perfect for dipping into creamy peanut butter.
He enjoyed the crunchy potato chips as he snacked on them during the movie.
The crunchy snow cracked under their boots as they walked.
He enjoyed the crunchy texture of the toasted sandwich.

senses.definition: (of food) having a hard or crisp covering or outer layer

examples.x: The bread had a crusty exterior and a soft, fluffy interior.
She enjoyed the crusty texture of the pizza's outer edge, known as the crust.
The pie had a golden-brown, crusty pastry that complemented the sweet filling.

senses.definition: easily harmed or destroyed

examples.x: The delicate flowers wilted in the hot sun.
Handle the delicate china teacups with care to avoid chipping.
The delicate glass ornaments shattered when they fell off the shelf.
The delicate artwork was protected behind glass in the museum.

senses.definition: having a soft and pliable quality reminiscent of raw or partially cooked dough, often characterized by a tender feel

examples.x: The cinnamon rolls were soft and doughy, with a gooey center of sweet glaze.
The pancakes were fluffy and doughy, making for a comforting breakfast experience.
The pizza crust was thick and doughy, offering a hearty and chewy bite.

senses.definition: (of drinks) carbonated and having bubbles of gas

examples.x: The fizzy soda provided a refreshing burst of carbonation on a hot day.
She enjoyed sipping on a fizzy lemonade, delighting in its tangy sweetness and effervescence.
The fizzy mineral water paired well with the spicy food, cooling the palate with its bubbles.
He poured a glass of fizzy champagne to celebrate the special occasion.
The fizzy kombucha was a popular choice among health-conscious consumers for its probiotic benefits.

senses.definition: having a texture that easily breaks into small, thin layers or pieces

examples.x: The croissant had a flaky texture, with each layer delicately separating upon a gentle touch.
The pie crust was perfectly flaky, providing a satisfying crunch with every bite.
The baked fish fillet had a flaky texture, easily falling apart with a fork.
The biscuits were soft on the inside and wonderfully flaky on the outside.
The chicken pot pie had a golden, flaky crust that encased a savory filling.

senses.definition: light and soft in texture, giving a feeling of coziness or warmth

examples.x: The cat's fur was fluffy and thick, making it irresistible to pet.
The clouds in the sky looked fluffy and white, like cotton candy.
The pancakes were fluffy and golden brown, with a light and airy texture.
The pillows on the bed were fluffy and plump, inviting a restful night's sleep.
The sweater was made from fluffy yarn, giving it a cozy and warm feel.

senses.definition: having a soft and sticky consistency
(of food) having a lot of liquid and tasting fresh or flavorful

examples.x: The juicy watermelon was refreshing on a hot summer day.
She cooked a juicy steak on the grill, perfectly seared on the outside and tender on the inside.
The oranges were juicy and ripe, perfect for making freshly squeezed juice.
The burger was juicy and flavorful, with the juices dripping down his chin as he took a bite.
The chef marinated the chicken in a flavorful sauce, resulting in juicy and tender meat.

senses.definition: having a fine and smooth surface that is pleasant to the touch

examples.x: Her hair felt silky after she applied the deep conditioner.
He admired the silky texture of the cream as it absorbed into his skin.
The kitten's fur was so silky that it was a pleasure to pet.
She loved the silky feel of the sheets on her bed, making it hard to get out in the morning.
The silky smooth texture of the lotion left her skin feeling soft and hydrated.

senses.definition: having a thick consistency that clings to surfaces when in contact

examples.x: The syrup was sticky and hard to clean off the counter.
She avoided the sticky caramel on the plate.
The honey had a sticky texture that coated everything it touched.
After melting, the chocolate became sticky and messy.
The jam was so sticky it clung to the spoon.

senses.definition: having a surface that is even and free from roughness or irregularities

examples.x: The marble countertop was smooth to the touch.
She admired the smooth finish of the wooden table.
The artist polished the sculpture until it was perfectly smooth.
The car’s paint job gave it a sleek and smooth appearance.
He ran his fingers over the smooth surface of the glass.

senses.definition: juicy and full of flavor
(of food) high in calories
easy to chew, swallow, and process in the digestive system

examples.x: The steamed vegetables were cooked to a perfect tenderness, making them easily digestible.
The rice cakes had a light and fluffy texture, providing a highly digestible snack.
The lean chicken breast was grilled to perfection, resulting in a tender and digestible protein.
The fruit salad featured ripe and juicy fruits, offering a refreshing and easily digestible treat.
The whole-grain bread was baked to a soft and chewy finish, promoting a digestible sandwich.

senses.definition: (of food or similar products)  containing little or no fat

examples.x: The store offers a wide range of fat-free yogurt options for health-conscious customers.
I switched to fat-free milk to reduce my calorie intake.
Many snack foods advertise themselves as fat-free, but they may still contain sugar.
She prefers fat-free salad dressings for a healthier alternative.
Fat-free snacks can sometimes lack flavor, but they are a good choice for those watching their weight.

senses.definition: (of food) likely to cause one to gain weight
(of food) making one's stomach feel full
resembling or containing flour, often being dry, powdery, or soft
(of substances) challenging for the body to break down and digest

examples.x: The doctor recommended avoiding indigestible fibers when suffering from a sensitive stomach.
While the dish was delicious, the excessive use of corn made it somewhat indigestible for me.

senses.definition: containing menthol, imparting a cooling and refreshing quality, often associated with medicinal or soothing properties

examples.x: The toothpaste had a mentholated formula for a fresh and cooling sensation
He enjoyed the mentholated flavor of the gum for a refreshing breath.
She used a mentholated balm to soothe her sore muscles.
The mentholated ointment provided relief for nasal congestion.
A mentholated cream was applied to ease the discomfort of insect bites.

senses.definition: containing a lot of milk or made primarily from milk

examples.x: The recipe called for a milky base, which gave the sauce its creamy texture.
The cheese was made from a milky substance, enhancing its rich flavor.
The dessert had a milky consistency, thanks to the generous amount of milk used.
The milky drink was a hit at the party, enjoyed by both kids and adults.

senses.definition: (of food) having a liquid or watery consistency

examples.x: The stew became overly soupy after simmering for an extended period.
The vegetable curry was pleasantly soupy, allowing the flavors to meld together.
The rice porridge had a comforting and soupy consistency, ideal for a warm breakfast.
Her homemade chili had a hearty and slightly soupy texture, perfect for dipping.

senses.definition: (of food) having tough strands that are hard to chew

examples.x: The chicken was overcooked and became stringy.
The steak was too tough and stringy to enjoy.
The soup had stringy pieces of celery that weren’t easy to swallow.
The pork had a stringy texture that made it hard to cut.
The roast beef was too stringy and chewy to eat comfortably.

senses.definition: (of food) high in carbohydrates and heavy, making one feel very full
having a texture that is dry, powdery, crumbly, and similar to chalk

examples.x: The cookie had a chalky texture due to excess flour in the batter.
The chocolate truffle had a velvety exterior but turned chalky inside.
The sunscreen left a chalky residue on the skin after application.
The dusty road felt chalky beneath the feet as they walked.
The crumbled feta cheese had a chalky texture, adding a savory element to the salad.

senses.definition: (of food or drink) containing a small amount of calories

examples.x: She prefers low-calorie snacks like yogurt and fruit.
He switched to low-calorie beverages to reduce his sugar intake.
